Class Tile

java.lang.Object
fr.uge.cascadia.structure.Tile
All Implemented Interfaces:
Lot
public final class Tile extends Object implements Lot
Represents a tile in the Cascadia game that contains specific habitats, associated animals, and supports rotation for gameplay mechanics.

The Tile class provides methods to manage habitats and animals, rotate the tile, and determine its compatibility with other game elements.

Structure of a Tile:

  • Each tile can have one or two habitats.
  • Each tile can support up to three animals.
  • Tiles are hexagonal and can be rotated.

  • Constructor Details

    • Tile

      public Tile(Habitat[] habitats, Animal[] animals, int rotation)
      Constructs a Tile with the specified habitats and animals.
      Parameters:
      habitats - an array of Habitat representing the habitats of the tile.
      animals - an array of Animal associated with the tile.
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if either habitats or animals is null.
      IllegalArgumentException - if the number of habitats is not 1 or 2, or the number of animals exceeds the allowed range (1-3).

  • Method Details

    • sides

      public Habitat[] sides(ImageLoader imageLoader)
      Generates the 6 sides of the hexagonal tile based on the current rotation.
      Returns:
      an array of 6 Habitat representing the sides of the tile.

    • calculateSides

      public Habitat[] calculateSides(ImageLoader imageLoader, int rotation)

    • rotate

      public void rotate()
      Rotates the tile clockwise by one position.

      Returns the current rotation of the tile.

      Retrieves the habitats associated with this tile.

      public Animal[] getAnimals()
      Retrieves the animals associated with this tile.

    • isTile

      public boolean isTile()
      Checks if this instance is a tile.
      Specified by:
      isTile in interface Lot
      Returns:
      true, as this class represents a tile.

    • isTileAnimalPair

      public boolean isTileAnimalPair()
      Checks if this instance is a tile-animal pair.
      Specified by:
      isTileAnimalPair in interface Lot
      Returns:
      false, as this class does not represent a tile-animal pair.

    • asTile

      public Tile asTile()
      Returns this instance as a Tile.
      Specified by:
      asTile in interface Lot
      Returns:
      this tile.

    • asTileAnimalPair

      public TileAnimalPair asTileAnimalPair()
      Throws an exception, as this instance cannot be cast to TileAnimalPair.
      Specified by:
      asTileAnimalPair in interface Lot
      Returns:
      nothing, always throws an exception.
      Throws:
      UnsupportedOperationException - if this method is called.

    • canAcceptAnimal

      public boolean canAcceptAnimal(Animal present)
      Checks if the tile can accept the specified animal.
      Specified by:
      canAcceptAnimal in interface Lot
      Parameters:
      present - the Animal to check.
      Returns:
      true if the tile can accept the animal; false otherwise.
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if present is null.

    • hasHabitat

      public boolean hasHabitat(ImageLoader imageLoader, Habitat habitat)
      Checks if the tile has the specified habitat.
      Specified by:
      hasHabitat in interface Lot
      Parameters:
      habitat - the Habitat to check.
      Returns:
      true if the tile contains the habitat; false otherwise.
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if habitat is null.

    • predefinedTiles

      public static Tile[] predefinedTiles()
      Generates a set of predefined tiles for the game with square tiles.
      Returns:
      an array of predefined Tile instances

    • idealTiles

      public static List<Tile> idealTiles()
      Generates a set of 25 ideal tiles for the game with hexagonal tiles.
      Returns:
      a list of ideal hexagonal Tile instances

    • hexTiles

      public static List<Tile> hexTiles()
      Generates a set of hexagonal tiles with two habitats and 2 or 3 allowed animals selected randomly for the game with hexagonal tiles.
      Returns:
      a list of hexagonal Tile instances
